proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months: each 1-unit increase in feature_080 is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.702 (p=0.00e+00); OLS slope = +0.1759 months per unit feature_080 (p=0.00e+00). Strong positive monotonic association explaining ~49% of variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_056=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_056=0 (binary marker, possibly an adverse-prognosis indicator).
Patients with feature_042=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_042=0 (binary marker, possibly a protective/treatment-benefit indicator).
Patients with feature_048=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_048=0 (binary marker, possibly an adverse-prognosis indicator).

analyses.result_summary: feature_056=1 mean PFS=3.617 (n=14969) vs =0 mean=5.153 (n=35031); difference=-1.536 months, Welch t-test p=0.00e+00.
feature_042=1 mean PFS=5.408 (n=17428) vs =0 mean=4.310 (n=32572); difference=+1.098 months, Welch t-test p=0.00e+00.
feature_048=1 mean PFS=3.750 (n=5090) vs =0 mean=4.800 (n=44910); difference=-1.050 months, Welch t-test p=1.45e-178.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 is an ordinal severity-like marker: pfs_months decreases monotonically as feature_063 goes from 0 to 1 to 2.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_063: 0→5.640 (n=17592), 1→4.444 (n=24971), 2→3.289 (n=7437). Monotonic decrease. Kruskal-Wallis H=5609, p=0.00e+00. OLS: feature_063=2 vs 0 = -2.351 mo (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_111=1 patients have longer pfs_months than feature_111=0 patients.
feature_015=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_015=0 patients.
feature_040=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_040=0 patients.
feature_039=1 patients have longer pfs_months than feature_039=0 patients.
feature_067 (continuous, 0-24.6) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_019 (continuous, 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months.
feature_101 (continuous, 1-100) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

After mutual adjustment in a single OLS model with feature_080, feature_063 (categorical), feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_039, feature_067, feature_019, and feature_101, all of these predictors retain independent and statistically significant associations with pfs_months in the same direction observed in their univariate analyses.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R²=0.792. Largest adjusted coefficients: C(feature_063)[T.2]=-2.335, feature_056=-1.549, C(feature_063)[T.1]=-1.189, feature_042=+1.102, feature_048=-0.984, feature_015=-0.582, feature_111=+0.531, feature_019=+0.476. All 12 predictors statistically significant (p<0.001) with directions consistent with univariate analyses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months differs across feature_063 strata: the magnitude of the feature_056 reduction in pfs_months becomes larger as feature_063 increases (i.e., feature_056 is more harmful in advanced feature_063=2 patients).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified means — f63=0: f56=0→6.12, f56=1→4.52; f63=1: f56=0→4.89, f56=1→3.39; f63=2: f56=0→3.74, f56=1→2.26. Interaction term feature_056:f63=1 = +0.066 (p=3.01e-02); feature_056:f63=2 = +0.119 (p=5.60e-03). Significant heterogeneity detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive (protective) effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is largest in feature_063=2 patients (advanced subgroup), reflecting greater absolute benefit in patients with worse baseline prognosis.
The negative effect of feature_048 on pfs_months becomes larger in absolute terms as feature_063 increases (i.e., interaction between feature_048 and stage).

The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months is modified by feature_080 (i.e., treatment-by-age interaction): the absolute reduction in pfs_months associated with feature_056=1 is different at low vs high values of feature_080.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_056:feature_080 = -0.0112 (p=3.13e-12). Stratified mean PFS — age3=low: f56=0→3.21, f56=1→1.77; age3=mid: f56=0→5.15, f56=1→3.55; age3=high: f56=0→7.14, f56=1→5.51.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is modified by feature_080: the magnitude of benefit varies across the range of feature_080.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_042:feature_080 = -0.0032 (p=4.38e-02). Stratified means — age3=low: f42=0→2.39, f42=1→3.52; age3=mid: f42=0→4.30, f42=1→5.35; age3=high: f42=0→6.26, f42=1→7.36.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combined presence of feature_056=1 and feature_042=1 yields pfs_months that is non-additive on the additive scale (interaction term in OLS is statistically significant), implying synergy or antagonism between these two markers.

analyses.result_summary: Joint mean pfs_months (after adjusting for feature_080 and feature_063): f56=0,f42=0:4.77; f56=0,f42=1:5.87; f56=1,f42=0:3.23; f56=1,f42=1:4.33. Interaction term f56:f42 = +0.0250 (p=3.57e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Unadjusted mean pfs_months differs across feature_011 (race) categories.

After adjustment for feature_080, feature_063 stage, and the principal binary prognostic markers (feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_039), pfs_months differs across feature_011 (race) categories — i.e., a residual disparity remains.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted contrasts vs feature_011='white': C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.asian]=+0.025 (p=0.29); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.black]=-0.001 (p=0.95); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.hispanic]=+0.007 (p=0.63); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.other]=+0.014 (p=0.71).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Unadjusted mean pfs_months differs across feature_089 (insurance) categories.
After adjustment for feature_080, feature_063 stage, and major binary prognostic markers, pfs_months differs across feature_089 (insurance) categories.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted means by feature_089: {'medicaid': 4.706401053979717, 'medicare': 4.692075376537438, 'private': 4.684984896296144, 'uninsured': 4.732867153700463}. Kruskal-Wallis H=1.22, p=0.748.

Adjusted contrasts vs feature_089='private': C(feature_089, Treatment(reference='private'))[T.medicaid]=+0.010 (p=0.55); C(feature_089, Treatment(reference='private'))[T.medicare]=+0.006 (p=0.59); C(feature_089, Treatment(reference='private'))[T.uninsured]=+0.008 (p=0.77).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_042 (positive prognostic marker) on pfs_months differs across feature_011 (race) categories — i.e., a treatment-by-race interaction is present.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified means by race × feature_042: asian: f42=0→4.37, f42=1→5.43; black: f42=0→4.34, f42=1→5.40; hispanic: f42=0→4.32, f42=1→5.43; other: f42=0→4.42, f42=1→5.43; white: f42=0→4.29, f42=1→5.40. Interaction terms vs white: feature_042:C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.asian]=-0.009 (p=0.88); feature_042:C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.black]=+0.036 (p=0.42); feature_042:C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.hispanic]=+0.010 (p=0.8); feature_042:C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.other]=-0.003 (p=0.98).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_042 on pfs_months differs across feature_089 (insurance) categories — i.e., an insurance-by-treatment interaction is present.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified means by insurance × feature_042: medicaid: f42=0→4.33, f42=1→5.41; medicare: f42=0→4.30, f42=1→5.41; private: f42=0→4.31, f42=1→5.40; uninsured: f42=0→4.35, f42=1→5.45. Interaction terms vs private: feature_042:C(feature_089, Treatment(reference='private'))[T.medicaid]=+0.065 (p=0.14); feature_042:C(feature_089, Treatment(reference='private'))[T.medicare]=+0.033 (p=0.29); feature_042:C(feature_089, Treatment(reference='private'))[T.uninsured]=+0.065 (p=0.37).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_063=0 (least advanced) patients, pfs_months differs across feature_011 (race) categories after adjustment for feature_080, feature_042, and feature_056.

analyses.result_summary: Stratum n=17592. Adjusted race contrasts vs white: C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.asian]=+0.033 (p=0.43); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.black]=+0.002 (p=0.95); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.hispanic]=+0.007 (p=0.8); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.other]=+0.045 (p=0.52).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_063=2 (most advanced) patients, pfs_months differs across feature_011 (race) categories after adjustment for feature_080, feature_042, and feature_056.

analyses.result_summary: Stratum n=7437. Adjusted race contrasts vs white: C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.asian]=+0.050 (p=0.44); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.black]=-0.026 (p=0.6); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.hispanic]=+0.000 (p=0.99); C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white'))[T.other]=-0.128 (p=0.24).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months is modified by feature_067 — i.e., the treatment effect of feature_056 differs at low vs high feature_067 values.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_056:feature_067 = -0.0022 (p=5.33e-01). Stratified means by feature_067 tertile × feature_056: f067=low: f56=0→5.42, f56=1→3.92; f067=mid: f56=0→5.21, f56=1→3.65; f067=high: f56=0→4.82, f56=1→3.27.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_087 (continuous, range 88-100, plausible performance-status-like score) is positively associated with pfs_months independently of feature_080 and feature_063.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate Pearson r=+0.0042 (p=0.35). Adjusted slope = -0.0023 mo per unit feature_087 (p=0.52).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (continuous, range 128-152) is independently associated with pfs_months after adjustment for feature_080 and feature_063 stage.
feature_088 (continuous, range 45-122, plausible diastolic blood pressure) is independently associated with pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_002 (continuous, range 40-130, plausible systolic blood pressure) is independently associated with pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_006 (continuous, 7.3-11.8) is independently associated with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate r=-0.0019 (p=0.67); adjusted slope=-0.0011 (p=0.65).
Univariate r=+0.0035 (p=0.43); adjusted slope=-0.0000 (p=0.99).
Univariate r=+0.0046 (p=0.3); adjusted slope=+0.0007 (p=0.22).
Univariate r=-0.0128 (p=0.0041); adjusted slope=-0.0336 (p=0.018).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_034=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than feature_034=0.
feature_077=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than feature_077=0.
feature_086=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than feature_086=0.
feature_055=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than feature_055=0.
feature_005=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than feature_005=0.
feature_031=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than feature_031=0.
The total count of positive binary indicators among the 69 non-strong binary features (a 'burden' score) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Burden range = 2-25 (mean 13.0). Univariate Pearson r=-0.0035 (p=0.44). After adjusting for feature_080 and feature_063: slope=-0.00102 mo per +1 burden (p=0.67).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergy/antagonism between feature_056 and feature_042 (interaction) is itself modified by feature_063 stage — i.e., a three-way interaction feature_056 × feature_042 × feature_063 is significant.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction terms: feature_056:feature_042:C(feature_063)[T.1]=+0.111 (p=0.065); feature_056:feature_042:C(feature_063)[T.2]=+0.035 (p=0.67). No significant 3-way interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final multivariable OLS model containing feature_080, feature_063, the top binary prognostic markers (feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_039), the top continuous biomarker-like features (feature_067, feature_019, feature_101, feature_087), and demographic factors (feature_011 race, feature_089 insurance), feature_080 and feature_063 remain the strongest independent predictors of pfs_months while race and insurance contribute negligible independent effects.

analyses.code: pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_111 + feature_015 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_087 + C(feature_011, Treatment(reference='white')) + C(feature_089, Treatment(reference='private'))
